visual: Arrays with Pictures: Same sticker sheet from 1.1 (3×4). System highlights rows and columns sequentially during Guide narration.
guide: "Now let me teach you the names for what you're seeing. Watch this."
visual_2: System highlights row 1 (top horizontal line of 4 stickers).
guide_2: "See this line going across? That's a **row**. Rows go across from left to right."
visual_3: System highlights row 2, then row 3 sequentially.
guide_3: "Row 1... row 2... row 3. Three rows, and they all go across."
visual_4: All row highlights clear. System highlights column 1 (left vertical line of 3 stickers).
guide_4: "Now watch this direction. See this line going up and down? That's a **column**. Columns go up and down."
visual_5: System highlights column 2, column 3, column 4 sequentially.
guide_5: "Column 1... column 2... column 3... column 4. Four columns, all going up and down."
visual_6: All highlights clear. Array returns to neutral display.
guide_6: "So think about the difference between rows and columns. How many rows that go across does this array have?"
on_correct: "3 rows — the lines that go across. So this array has 3 rows and 4 columns. An array is equal groups organized into rows and columns."
vocabulary: **row** ("rows go across — left to right"), **column** ("columns go up and down") — formally introduced here.
